mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or flat, the discount rate on future earnings declines and equity duration rises. This supports higher S&P 500 returns over the next month because valuation expansion dominates, especially when inflation is not re-accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with non-increasing FEDFUNDS, or if the signal turns negative during easing periods for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend marks weakening labor income and recession risk. That shift reduces earnings expectations and risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after the threshold is crossed.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if threshold-crossing months are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average 20-day return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales and housing starts both strengthen, household demand is broadening across goods and housing. That combination supports revenue growth expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint-positive RSAFS and HOUST regimes do not outperform the unconditional 60-day SPX return by at least 150 bps annualized, or if the sign is negative in a majority of such windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL indicates output gains without fresh inflation pressure. That mix improves expected margins and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average while CPIAUCSL inflation stays below its prior 6-month pace
failure_condition: Reject if expansion-with-disinflation episodes fail to produce positive abnormal 20-day returns, or if the sign reverses when CPIAUCSL is stable.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level above the neutral post-2008 range tightens financial conditions and compresses equity multiples. In that regime, higher policy rates predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as valuation repricing dominates.
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not negative on average after FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or if the trigger produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized slowdown in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals weakening final demand, which should reduce earnings revisions and pressure equity prices over the next 60 trading days. The effect is strongest when both series are decelerating together.
failure_condition: Reject if joint deceleration in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not produce below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the sign without interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A declining PAYEMS series with a rising UNRATE prints an employment deterioration shock. That combination signals lower household income growth and higher recession probability, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE rises in the same period
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor deterioration trigger is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the interaction term is not stronger than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST during a period of elevated CPIAUCSL indicates weaker construction activity and tighter real activity conditions. That mix predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days because growth expectations and cyclical sentiment deteriorate together.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month average while CPIAUCSL is above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the housing-down, inflation-up trigger does not yield negative 5-day abnormal returns, or if observed returns are positive in a majority of triggered instances.
